name: Interior lighting grid
description: A general lighting density rule that guarantees no interior cell is more than seven blocks of Manhattan distance from a light source, producing Alexander's pools of light without leaving any room dim. The same pattern adapts to medieval lanterns hanging from a beam, modern recessed glowstone, Japanese paper-shaded floor lamps, and rustic wall torches — what varies is the lamp block, what stays constant is the density and arrangement. Applies to residential, tavern, temple, monument, and shop interior rooms of all sizes. The grid scales with room footprint: small rooms (≤5×5) get one center lamp; medium rooms (5-10 across) get one corner and one center; large rooms (>10) get a regular 5×5 grid of lamps.
placement_rules: Place ⌈area / 25⌉ lamp blocks, minimum 1 per room.
For rooms with ceiling ≥ 4 cells high, hang lanterns 1 cell below ceiling on a grid.
For shorter rooms (3 high), use wall-mounted torches at y=floor.y0 + 2.
Lamps spaced so every floor cell is within 7-block Manhattan distance of one (target: zero dark voxels).
Material choice per style: lantern (medieval/rustic/gothic/renaissance), sea_lantern or glowstone (modern), spruce_planks+torch combo (japanese paper-lantern emulation).
